name: class
detail: Define a traffic classification match criteria for the specified class-map name.

params.name: class-default
params.description: Refers to a system default class that matches unclassified packets.

usageSummary: Before using the class command, you must use the policy-map global configuration command to identify the policy map and enter policy-map configuration mode.  After specifying a policy map, you can configure a policy for new classes or modify a policy for any existing classes in that policy map.  You attach the policy map to a port by using the service-policy interface configuration command.

name: class-map
detail: Create a class map to be used for matching packets to the class whose name you specify and to enter class-map configuration mode.

params.name: match-any
params.description: (Optional) Performs a logical-OR of the matching statements under this class map. One or more criteria must be matched.

params.name: type
params.description: (Optional) Configures the CPL class map. class-map-name Name of the class for the class map. The class name is used for both the class map and to configure a policy for the class in the policy map.

usageSummary: The class-map command and its subcommands are used to define packet classification, marking, and aggregate policing as part of a globally named service policy applied on a per-port basis.  After you are in quality of service (QoS) class-map configuration mode, these configuration commands are available: • description—Describes the class map (up to 200 characters).  The show class-map privileged EXEC command displays the description and the name of the class map.

name: debug qos
detail: Enable debugging of the quality of service (QoS) software.
syntax: debug qos {capability | command-installation-time | events | index | pre-classify | provision | service-policy | set | snmp | tunnel_marking} service-policy | set | snmp | tunnel_marking}
noForm: no debug qos {capability | command-installation-time | events | index | pre-classify | provision |

params.name: capability
params.description: Displays all QoS capability debug messages. command-installation-time Displays the amount of time the QoS command takes to become effective.

params.name: events
params.description: Displays QoS MQC events.

params.name: index
params.description: Displays class-based QoS MIB index persistency.

params.name: pre-classify
params.description: Displays QoS pre-classify events for VPN.

params.name: provision
params.description: Displays QoS provisions.

params.name: service-policy
params.description: Displays QoS service policies.

params.name: set
params.description: Displays QoS packet marking.

params.name: snmp
params.description: Displays class-based QoS configuration and statistics information.

params.name: tunnel_marking
params.description: Displays QoS packet tunnel marking.

usageSummary: The undebug qos command is the same as the no debug qos command.  When you enable debugging on a switch stack, it is enabled only on the primary stack.  To enable debugging on a stack member, you can start a session from the primary stack by using the session switch-number privileged EXEC command, then enter the debug command at the command-line prompt of the stack member.

name: match
detail: Define the match criteria to classify traffic.
syntax: match {access-group acl-index-or-name | ip {dscp dscp-list | precedence ip-precedence-list} | protocol {arp | cdp | http | ip | ipv6}} protocol {arp | cdp | http | ip | ipv6}}
noForm: no match {access-group acl-index-or-name | ip {dscp dscp-list | precedence ip-precedence-list} |

params.name: access-group
params.description: Specifies the number or name of an access control list (ACL). acl-index-or-name The range is from 1 to 2799.

params.name: ip
params.description: Sets IP specific values. • dscp dscp-list—Lists up to eight IP Differentiated Services Code Point (DSCP) values to match against incoming packets. Separate each value with a space. The range is 0 to 63. You also can enter a mnemonic name for a commonly used value. • precedence ip-precedence-list—Lists up to eight IP-precedence values to match against incoming packets. Separate each value with a space. The range is 0 to 7. You also can enter a mnemonic name for a commonly used value.

params.name: protocol
params.description: Specifies the name of a protocol to be used as the match criteria against which packets are checked to determine if they belong to the class specified by the class map. The following protocols are supported: arp,cdp, http, ip, and ipv6.

usageSummary: The match command is used to specify which fields in the incoming packets are examined to classify the packets.  Only the IP access group or the MAC access group matching to the Ether Type/Len are supported.  If you enter the class-map match-any class-map-name global configuration command, you can enter the following match commands: • match access-group name acl-name • match ip dscp dscp-list • match ip precedence ip-precedence-list You cannot enter the match access-group acl-index command.

name: mls qos
detail: Enable quality of service (QoS) for the entire switch.
usageSummary: When the mls qos command is entered, QoS is enabled with the default parameters on all ports in the system.  QoS must be globally enabled to use QoS classification, policing, marking or dropping, queueing, and traffic shaping features.  You can create a policy map and attach it to a port before entering the mls qos command.

name: mls qos aggregate-policer
detail: Define policer parameters that can be shared by multiple classes within the same policy map.
syntax: mls qos aggregate-policer aggregate-policer-name rate-bps burst-byte exceed-action {drop | policed-dscp-transmit}
noForm: no mls qos aggregate-policer aggregate-policer-name rate-bps burst-byte {drop | policed-dscp-transmit}

params.name: aggregate-policer-name
params.description: The name of the aggregate policer as referenced by the police aggregate policy-map class configuration command.

params.name: rate-bps
params.description: The average traffic rate in bits per second (b/s). The range is 8000 to 10000000000.

params.name: burst-byte
params.description: The normal burst size in bytes. The range is 8000 to 1000000.

params.name: exceed-action drop
params.description: Sets the traffic rate. If the rate is exceeded, the switch drops the packet.

params.name: exceed-action
params.description: Sets the traffic rate. If the rate is exceeded, the switch changes the

params.name: policed-dscp-transmit
params.description: Differentiated Services Code Point (DSCP) of the packet to that specified in the policed-DSCP map and then sends the packet.

usageSummary: A policer defines a maximum permissible rate of transmission, a maximum burst size for transmissions, and an action to take if either maximum is exceeded.  Define an aggregate policer if the policer is shared with multiple classes.  Policers for a port cannot be shared with other policers for another port; traffic from two different ports cannot be aggregated for policing purposes.

name: mls qos cos
detail: Define the default class of service (CoS) value of a port or to assign the default CoS to all incoming packets on the port.
usageSummary: You can use the default value to assign a CoS and Differentiated Services Code Point (DSCP) value to all incoming packets that are untagged (if the incoming packet does not have a CoS value).  You also can assign a default CoS and DSCP value to all incoming packets by using the override keyword.  Use the override keyword when all incoming packets on certain ports deserve higher or lower priority than packets entering from other ports.

name: mls qos dscp-mutation
detail: Apply a Differentiated Services Code Point (DSCP)-to-DSCP-mutation map to a DSCP-trusted port.
usageSummary: If two quality of service (QoS) domains have different DSCP definitions, use the DSCP-to-DSCP-mutation map to translate one set of DSCP values to match the definition of another domain.  You apply the DSCP-to-DSCP-mutation map to the receiving port (ingress mutation) at the boundary of a QoS administrative domain.  With ingress mutation, the new DSCP value overwrites the one in the packet, and QoS handles the packet with this new value.

name: mls qos map
detail: Define the DSCP-to-DSCP-mutation map.
syntax: mls qos map {dscp-mutation dscp-mutation-name in-dscp to out-dscp}
noForm: no mls qos map {dscp-mutation dscp-mutation-name in-dscp to out-dscp}

params.name: dscp-mutation
params.description: Defines the DSCP-to-DSCP-mutation map. dscp-mutation-name in-dscp to For dscp-mutation-name, enter the mutation map name. out-dscp For in-dscp, enter up to eight DSCP values, with each value separated by a space, then enter the to keyword. For out-dscp, enter a single DSCP value. The range is 0 to 63.

usageSummary: All the maps are globally defined.  The DSCP-to-DSCP-mutation map is applied to a specific port.  This example shows how to define the DSCP-to-DSCP-mutation map.

name: mls qos queue-set output buffers
detail: Allocate buffers to a queue set of four egress queues per port.
syntax: mls qos queue-set output qset-id buffers allocation1 ... allocation4
noForm: no mls qos queue-set output qset-id buffers

params.name: qset-id
params.description: Queue set ID. Each port belongs to a queue set, which defines all the characteristics of the four egress queues per port. The range is 1 to 2.

params.name: allocation1 ...
params.description: Buffer space allocation (percentage) for each queue (four values for queues 1 to

params.name: allocation4
params.description: 4). For allocation1, allocation3, and allocation4, the range is 0 to 99. For allocation2, the range is 1 to 100 (including the CPU buffer). Separate each value with a space.

usageSummary: Specify the allocation values, and separate each with a space.  Allocate buffers according to the importance of the traffic.  For example, give a large percentage of the buffer to the queue with the highest-priority traffic.

name: mls qos queue-set output threshold
detail: Configure the weighted tail-drop (WTD) thresholds, to guarantee the availability of buffers, and to configure the maximum memory allocation to a queue set (four egress queues per port).
syntax: mls qos queue-set output qset-id threshold [queue-id ] drop-threshold1 drop-threshold2 reserved-threshold maximum-threshold
noForm: no mls qos queue-set output qset-id threshold [queue-id ]

params.name: qset-id
params.description: Queue set ID. Each port belongs to a queue set, which defines all the characteristics of the four egress queues per port. The range is 1 to 2.

params.name: queue-id
params.description: (Optional) The queue in the queue set on which the command is performed. The range is 1 to 4.

params.name: drop-threshold1
params.description: Two WTD thresholds expressed as a percentage of the allocated memory of

params.name: drop-threshold2
params.description: the queue. The range is 1 to 3200 percent.

params.name: reserved-threshold
params.description: The amount of memory to be guaranteed (reserved) for the queue and expressed as a percentage of the allocated memory. The range is 1 to 100 percent.

params.name: maximum-threshold
params.description: Queue in the full condition that is enabled to get more buffers than are reserved for it. This is the maximum memory the queue can have before the packets are dropped. The range is 1 to 3200 percent.

usageSummary: Use the mls qos queue-set output qset-id buffers global configuration command to allocate a fixed number of buffers to the four queues in a queue set.  Table 25: Default Egress Queue WTD Threshold Settings Feature Queue 1 Queue 2 Queue 3 Queue 4 WTD drop threshold 100 200 100 100 1 percent percent percent percent Consolidated Platform Command Reference, Cisco IOS Release 15. 2(6)E (Catalyst 2960-X Switches) 379 QoS mls qos queue-set output threshold Feature Queue 1 Queue 2 Queue 3 Queue 4 WTD drop threshold 100 200 100 100 2 percent percent percent percent Reserved threshold 50 percent 100 50 percent 50 percent percent Maximum threshold 400 400 400 400 percent percent percent percent The drop-threshold percentages can exceed 100 percent and can be up to the maximum (if the maximum threshold exceeds 100 percent).

name: mls qos rewrite ip dscp
detail: Configure the switch to change or rewrite the Differentiated Services Code Point (DSCP) field of an incoming IP packet.
noForm: no mls qos rewrite ip dscp
usageSummary: DSCP transparency affects only the DSCP field of a packet at the egress.  If DSCP transparency is enabled by using the no mls qos rewrite ip dscp command, the switch does not modify the DSCP field in the incoming packet, and the DSCP field in the outgoing packet is the same as that in the incoming packet.

name: mls qos srr-queue output cos-map
detail: Map class of service (CoS) values to an egress queue or to map CoS values to a queue and to a threshold ID.
syntax: mls qos srr-queue output cos-map queue queue-id {cos1 ... cos8 | threshold threshold-id cos1 ... cos8 }
noForm: no mls qos srr-queue output cos-map

params.name: queue queue-id
params.description: Specifies a queue number. For queue-id, the range is 1 to 4.

params.name: cos1 ... cos8
params.description: CoS values that are mapped to an egress queue. For cos1...cos8, enter up to eight values, and separate each value with a space. The range is 0 to 7.

params.name: threshold threshold-id
params.description: Maps CoS values to a queue threshold ID. cos1...cos8 For threshold-id, the range is 1 to 3. For cos1...cos8, enter up to eight values, and separate each value with a space. The range is 0 to 7.

usageSummary: The drop-threshold percentage for threshold 3 is predefined.  It is set to the queue-full state.

name: mls qos srr-queue output dscp-map
detail: Map Differentiated Services Code Point (DSCP) values to an egress queue or to map DSCP values to a queue and to a threshold ID.
syntax: mls qos srr-queue output dscp-map queue queue-id { dscp1 ... dscp8 | threshold threshold-id dscp1 ... dscp8 }
noForm: no mls qos srr-queue output dscp-map

params.name: queue queue-id
params.description: Specifies a queue number. For queue-id, the range is 1 to 4.

params.name: dscp1 ... dscp8
params.description: DSCP values that are mapped to an egress queue. For dscp1...dscp8, enter up to eight values, and separate each value with a space. The range is 0 to 63.

params.name: threshold threshold-id
params.description: Maps DSCP values to a queue threshold ID. dscp1...dscp8 For threshold-id, the range is 1 to 3. For dscp1...dscp8, enter up to eight values, and separate each value with a space. The range is 0 to 63.

usageSummary: The drop-threshold percentage for threshold 3 is predefined.  It is set to the queue-full state.  For default DSCP output queue-threshold map values, see Table 27: Default DSCP Output Queue Threshold Map, on page 387.

name: mls qos trust
detail: Configure the port trust state.
syntax: mls qos trust [{cos | device {cisco-phone | cts | ip-camera | media-player} | dscp | ip-precedence}]
noForm: no mls qos trust [{cos | device {cisco-phone | cts | ip-camera | media-player} | dscp | ip-precedence}]

params.name: cos
params.description: (Optional) Classifies an ingress packet by using the packet CoS value. For an untagged packet, use the port default CoS value.

params.name: device cisco-phone
params.description: (Optional) Classifies an ingress packet by trusting the CoS or DSCP value sent from the Cisco IP Phone (trusted boundary), depending on the trust setting. device {cts | ip-camera (Optional) Classifies an ingress packet by trusting the CoS or DSCP value for these

params.name: | media-player}
params.description: video devices: • cts—Cisco TelePresence System • ip-camera—Cisco IP camera • media-player—Cisco digital media player For an untagged packet, use the port default CoS value.

params.name: dscp
params.description: (Optional) Classifies an ingress packet by using the packet DSCP value (most significant 6 bits of 8-bit service-type field). For a non-IP packet, the packet CoS is used if the packet is tagged. For an untagged packet, the default port CoS value is used.

params.name: ip-precedence
params.description: (Optional) Classifies an ingress packet by using the packet IP-precedence value (most significant 3 bits of 8-bit service-type field). For a non-IP packet, the packet CoS is used if the packet is tagged. For an untagged packet, the port default CoS value is used.

usageSummary: Packets entering a quality of service (QoS) domain are classified at the edge of the domain.  When the packets are classified at the edge, the switch port within the QoS domain can be configured to one of the trusted states because there is no need to classify the packets at every switch within the domain.  Use this command to specify whether the port is trusted and which fields of the packet to use to classify traffic.

name: police
detail: Define a policer for classified traffic.

params.name: rate-bps
params.description: Specifies the average traffic rate in bits per second (b/s). The range is 8000 to 10000000000.

params.name: burst-byte
params.description: Specifies the normal burst size in bytes. The range is 8000 to 1000000.

params.name: exceed-action drop
params.description: (Optional) Sets the traffic rate. If the rate is exceeded, the switch drops the packet .

params.name: exceed-action
params.description: (Optional) Sets the traffic rate. If the rate is exceeded, the switch changes

params.name: policed-dscp-transmit
params.description: the Differentiated Services Code Point (DSCP) of the packet to that specified in the policed-DSCP map and then sends the packet.

params.name: aggregate
params.description: Chooses the aggregate policer for the current class.

usageSummary: A policer defines a maximum permissible rate of transmission, a maximum burst size for transmissions, and an action to take if either maximum is exceeded.  The port ASIC device, which controls more than one physical port, supports 256 policers on the switch (255 user-configurable policers plus 1 policer reserved for internal use).  The maximum number of configurable policers supported per port is 63.

name: police aggregate
detail: Apply an aggregate policer to multiple classes in the same policy map.

params.name: aggregate-policer-name
params.description: The name of the aggregate policer.

usageSummary: A policer defines a maximum permissible rate of transmission, a maximum burst size for transmissions, and an action to take if either maximum is exceeded.  The port ASIC device, which controls more than one physical port, supports 256 policers on the switch (255 user-configurable policers plus 1 policer reserved for internal use).  The maximum number of configurable policers supported per port is 63.

name: policy map
detail: Create or modify a policy map that can be attached to multiple physical ports and to enter policy-map configuration mode.
usageSummary: After entering the policy-map command, you enter policy-map configuration mode, and these configuration commands are available: • class—Defines the classification match criteria for the specified class map.  • description—Describes the policy map (up to 200 characters).  • exit—Exits policy-map configuration mode and returns you to global configuration mode.

name: queue-set
detail: Map a port to a queue set.
usageSummary: For information about automatic generation of the queue-set ID with the auto qos voip command, see the “Usage Guidelines” section for the auto qos voip, on page 346 command.

name: service-policy
detail: Apply a policy map to the input of a physical port.

params.name: input
params.description: Applies the specified policy map to the input of a physical port. policy-map-name

usageSummary: Though visible in the command-line help strings, the output keyword is not supported.  Policy maps can be configured on physical ports.  A policy map is defined by the policy map command.

name: set
detail: Classify IP traffic by setting a Differentiated Services Code Point (DSCP) or an IP-precedence value in the packet.
syntax: set {dscp new-dscp | ip {dscp | precedence} | precedence precedence}
noForm: no set {dscp new-dscp | ip {dscp | precedence} | precedence precedence}

params.name: dscp new-dscp
params.description: Sets the DSCP value in IPv4 and IPv6 packets. The range is 0 to 63. ip {dscp | precedence } Sets the IP values. • dscp—Sets the IP DSCP value. • precedence—Sets the IP precedence value. precedence new-precedence Sets the precedence in IPv4 and IPv6 packets. The range is 0 to 7.

usageSummary: If you have used the set ip dscp policy-map class configuration command, the device changes this command to set dscp in the device configuration.  If you enter the set ip dscp policy-map class configuration command, this setting appears as set dscp in the device configuration.  You can use the set ip precedence policy-map class configuration command or the set precedence policy-map class configuration command.

name: show class-map
detail: Display quality of service (QoS) class maps, which define the match criteria to classify traffic.
syntax: show class-map [class-map-name | type control subscriber {all | class-map-name}]

params.name: class-map-name
params.description: (Optional) Class map name. type control subscriber (Optional) Displays information about control class maps.

params.name: all
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about all control class maps.

usageSummary: This command is supported only on the LAN Base image.

name: show mls qos
detail: Display global quality of service (QoS) configuration information.

name: show mls qos aggregate-policer
detail: Display the quality of service (QoS) aggregate policer configuration.

params.name: aggregate-policer-name
params.description: (Optional) Displays the policer configuration for the specified name.

usageSummary: A policer defines a maximum permissible rate of transmission, a maximum burst size for transmissions, and an action to take if either maximum is exceeded.  This command is supported only on the LAN Base image.

name: show mls qos interface
detail: Display quality of service (QoS) information at the port level.
syntax: show mls qos interface [interface-id] [{buffers | queueing | statistics}]

params.name: interface-id
params.description: (Optional) The QoS information for the specified port. Valid interfaces include physical ports.

params.name: buffers
params.description: (Optional) Displays the buffer allocation among the queues.

params.name: queueing
params.description: (Optional) Displays the queueing strategy (shared or shaped) and the weights corresponding to the queues.

params.name: statistics
params.description: (Optional) Displays statistics for sent and received Differentiated Services Code Points (DSCPs) and class of service (CoS) values, the number of packets enqueued or dropped per egress queue, and the number of in-profile and out-of-profile packets for each policer.

usageSummary: Though visible in the command-line help string, the policers keyword is not supported.  This command is supported only on the LAN Base image.

name: show mls qos maps
detail: Display quality of service (QoS) mapping information.
syntax: show mls qos maps [{cos-dscp | cos-output-q | dscp-cos | dscp-mutation dscp-mutation-name | dscp-output-q | ip-prec-dscp | policed-dscp}]

params.name: cos-dscp
params.description: (Optional) Displays class of service (CoS)-to-DSCP map.

params.name: cos-output-q
params.description: (Optional) Displays the CoS output queue threshold map.

params.name: dscp-cos
params.description: (Optional) Displays DSCP-to-CoS map. dscp-mutation dscp-mutation-name (Optional) Displays the specified DSCP-to-DSCP-mutation map.

params.name: dscp-output-q
params.description: (Optional) Displays the DSCP output queue threshold map.

params.name: ip-prec-dscp
params.description: (Optional) Displays the IP-precedence-to-DSCP map.

params.name: policed-dscp
params.description: (Optional) Displays the policed-DSCP map.

usageSummary: During classification, QoS uses the mapping tables to represent the priority of the traffic and to derive a corresponding class of service (CoS) or Differentiated Services Code Point (DSCP) value from the received CoS, DSCP, or IP precedence value.  The policed-DSCP, DSCP-to-CoS, and the DSCP-to-DSCP-mutation maps appear as a matrix.  The d1 column specifies the most-significant digit in the DSCP.

name: show mls qos queue-set
detail: Display quality of service (QoS) settings for the egress queues.

name: show policy-map
detail: Display quality of service (QoS) policy maps, which define classification criteria for incoming traffic.

params.name: policy-map-name
params.description: (Optional) The policy map name.

usageSummary: Policy maps can include policers that specify the bandwidth limitations and the action to take if the limits are exceeded.

name: srr-queue bandwidth limit
detail: Limit the maximum output on a port.

params.name: weight1
params.description: The port speed limit in percentage terms. The range is 10 to 90.

usageSummary: If you configure this command to 80 percent, the port is idle 20 percent of the time.  The line rate drops to 80 percent of the connected speed.  These values are not exact because the hardware adjusts the line rate in increments of six.

name: srr-queue bandwidth shape
detail: Assign the shaped weights and to enable bandwidth shaping on the four egress queues mapped to a port.
syntax: srr-queue bandwidth shape weight1 weight2 weight3 weight4

params.name: weight1 weight2 weight3
params.description: The weights that specify the percentage of the port that is shaped. The inverse

params.name: weight4
params.description: ratio (1/weight) specifies the shaping bandwidth for this queue. Separate each value with a space. The range is 0 to 65535.

usageSummary: In shaped mode, the queues are guaranteed a percentage of the bandwidth, and they are rate-limited to that amount.  Shaped traffic does not use more than the allocated bandwidth even if the link is idle.  Use shaping to smooth bursty traffic or to provide a smoother output over time.

name: srr-queue bandwidth share
detail: Assign the shared weights and to enable bandwidth sharing on the four egress queues mapped to a port.
syntax: srr-queue bandwidth share weight1 weight2 weight3 weight4

params.name: weight1 weight2 weight3
params.description: The ratios of weight1, weight2, weight3, and weight4 specify the ratio of

params.name: weight4
params.description: the frequency in which the SRR scheduler dequeues packets. Separate each value with a space. The range is 1 to 255.

usageSummary: The ratio of the weights is the ratio of frequency in which the shaped round-robin (SRR) scheduler dequeues packets from each queue.  The absolute value of each weight is meaningless, and only the ratio of parameters is used.  In shared mode, the queues share the bandwidth among them according to the configured weights.

name: trust
detail: Define a trust state for traffic classified through the class policy-map configuration or the class-map global configuration command.

params.name: cos
params.description: (Optional) Classifies an ingress packet by using the packet class of service (CoS) value. For an untagged packet, the port default CoS value is used.

params.name: dscp
params.description: (Optional) Classifies an ingress packet by using the packet Differentiated Services Code Point (DSCP) values (most significant 6 bits of 8-bit service-type field). For a non-IP packet, the packet CoS value is used if the packet is tagged. If the packet is untagged, the default port CoS value is used to map CoS to DSCP. ip-precedence (Optional) Classifies an ingress packet by using the packet IP-precedence value (most significant 3 bits of 8-bit service-type field). For a non-IP packet, the packet CoS value is used if the packet is tagged. If the packet is untagged, the port default CoS value is used to map CoS to DSCP.

usageSummary: Use this command to distinguish the quality of service (QoS) trust behavior for certain traffic from other traffic.  For example, incoming traffic with certain DSCP values can be trusted.  You can configure a class map to match and trust the DSCP values in the incoming traffic.
